passage.title: School Map Update
passage.text: The hallway guide team hoped younger students could follow the morning plan faster. At first, the helpers used a wall plan with many decorated arrows, but the chart looked cheerful but felt crowded. The youngest class said "I like the colors, but I still do not know where to turn." After hearing that, the team cut the long labels into short station names and spread the directions farther apart on the wall. They also put quick-check boards near the busiest doors so the signs gave one more clear clue before each change. On the next test day, almost every student finished the station walk more quickly than before. The team noticed that students needed fewer words and stronger visual clues. Because of that result, the helpers chose to use the new sign system in more grade-one areas.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Making a school path easier for younger students to use
Painting a room with brighter colors
Buying more books for a library sale
Writing long reports about class rules
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: The labels were too small to read quickly
The first plan had too many steps
Students could not tell what to do next
The chart felt too crowded to scan quickly
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the helpers put near the busiest doors?
choices: Short signs
Color blocks
Quick-check boards
Larger arrows
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does decoration mean in the seventh sentence?
choices: A person who leads the school
Pretty-looking parts that are not the main help
A room that is easy to clean
A rule about lunch time
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: Students could understand the next move more quickly
It made the room much larger
It reduced the number of students
It removed every sign from the area
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Use the same clearer style in more places
Remove every sign from the room
Ask students to memorize everything
Expand the new sign system to other grade-one spaces
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
